summary: Legendary rain‑soaked show captured with excellent audience recording – thunder‑driven performances of Wharf Rat, Sugar Magnolia and The Other One shine.
review: Fans describe this June 20 1983 Merriweather Post Pavilion concert as an almost mythic event: relentless storm, lightning strikes that cut the PA, and a second set that rode the chaos with ferocious energy. The audience‑tape source is praised as the best available – clear, full‑bodied and surprisingly detailed despite occasional speed/pitch quirks – and it preserves standout moments like Wharf Rat, Sugar Magnolia, The Other One, Truckin' and a soaring Baby Blue encore.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly enthusiastic 5‑star reviews highlight an exceptional performance and a high‑quality audience source; only isolated technical complaints lower the score slightly.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, full‑range capture from lawn microphones; praised as the best source despite occasional speed/pitch anomalies and brief PA drop‑outs caused by lightning.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set solid and energetic; second set turned into a storm‑driven marathon with power outages, lightning strikes, and the band feeding off the chaos, culminating in an unforgettable encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Guitar tone repeatedly called perfect; one reviewer noted he sounded nervous after a lightning strike and another mentioned pitch issues (chipmunk effect).
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass ‘bombs’ on The Other One praised; several reviewers say Phil was “challenging the thunder gods” with powerful low‑end throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Improvisational highlights, especially his “Bob Star” moments in Sugar Magnolia and spoken “Aborigine rap” during Sugar Mag, are frequently lauded.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s keyboard work gets special shout‑outs – his solo on Rooster described as storm‑like and a key part of the show’s intensity.
